$comment: NON-NORMATIVE. This vocabulary is a conformance-suite diagnostic aid, not part of the CDX specification. The specification mandates DISPOSITIONS (State Machine section 5.4) and lineage OUTCOMES (Provenance and Lineage section 3.3) — never error identifiers. A conforming implementation is free to use its own internal errors and map them to these codes in its conformance adapter; it is never required to adopt this vocabulary. A code sharpens a failure report and is never by itself the pass criterion for a MUST — the normative assertion is the `disposition` each entry records.

compatibilityPolicy.summary: Codes are append-only and permanently stable once published.
rules: A code is NEVER re-pointed at a different defect. If the meaning must change, a new code is added and the old one is deprecated.
A deprecated code keeps its entry forever, gaining `deprecated: true` and, where applicable, `supersededBy`. Entries are never deleted.
`version` is the vocabulary's own semantic version and bumps on any addition or deprecation.
`specVersion` is the CDX specification version the `clause` citations resolve against.

dispositionNote: `disposition` records the load-time disposition the SPECIFICATION assigns to this defect class (State Machine section 5.4), with `dispositionClause` naming the row relied on. It is the normative half of an entry. A null disposition means the specification assigns no document-level disposition to this defect — not that a consumer may ignore it. NOTE for lineage entries: the lineage OUTCOME vocabulary (VERIFIED/INCOMPLETE/REJECTED, Provenance and Lineage section 3.3) is a different axis from the DISPOSITION vocabulary (IGNORE/WARNING/INTEGRITY-ERROR/REJECT). A lineage outcome of REJECTED does NOT mean the disposition REJECT: a proven-forged lineage is a WARNING, because the document's content identity is unaffected and it is not blocked on lineage grounds.

CDX-E-MANIFEST-ID-PENDING.summary: A manifest projection was requested for a document whose id is still `pending`.
CDX-E-MANIFEST-ID-PENDING.dispositionClause: Not a document defect: the manifest is well-formed and the projection is simply undefined until the document id is assigned. No load-time disposition applies.
CDX-E-MANIFEST-ID-PENDING.note: The projection is defined only once the document id is fixed.

CDX-E-MANIFEST-HASH-MALFORMED.summary: A declared content hash is absent, or is not a well-formed `algorithm:hexdigest`.
CDX-E-MANIFEST-HASH-MALFORMED.dispositionClause: State Machine section 5.4.2 (manifest absent, unparseable, or missing or mistyping a required field)

CDX-E-MANIFEST-REFERENCE-MALFORMED.summary: A declared file reference is not a well-formed `{path, hash}` object (not an object, or a missing/non-string path).
CDX-E-MANIFEST-REFERENCE-MALFORMED.dispositionClause: State Machine section 5.4.2 (manifest absent, unparseable, or missing or mistyping a required field)
CDX-E-MANIFEST-REFERENCE-MALFORMED.note: Distinct from CDX-E-MANIFEST-HASH-MALFORMED: the defect is the shape of the reference, not the hash value.

CDX-E-MANIFEST-PATH-TRAVERSAL.summary: A declared path is not a safe archive-relative path (escapes the archive root).
CDX-E-MANIFEST-PATH-TRAVERSAL.clause: Security Extension section 9.7; Container Format section 9.1
CDX-E-MANIFEST-PATH-TRAVERSAL.dispositionClause: State Machine section 5.4.3 (an unsafe archive path or symlink always REJECTs, in every state)

CDX-E-MANIFEST-VERSION-MALFORMED.dispositionClause: State Machine section 5.4.2 (manifest absent, unparseable, or missing or mistyping a required field)

CDX-E-MANIFEST-STATE-UNKNOWN.clause: State Machine section 3.1; Security Extension section 9.7
CDX-E-MANIFEST-STATE-UNKNOWN.dispositionClause: State Machine section 5.4.2 (a `state` value outside the defined lifecycle enum, so the mutability-and-signature contract cannot be established)

CDX-E-MANIFEST-EXTENSION-DUPLICATE.summary: Two `manifest.extensions[]` entries share an id, making the projection ambiguous.
CDX-E-MANIFEST-EXTENSION-DUPLICATE.dispositionClause: State Machine section 5.4.2 assigns NO row to this defect class. The projection fails closed on it (the projected bytes would otherwise be ambiguous), but the specification does not state a load-time disposition and none is inferred here — notably the generic "missing or mistyping a required field" row does not reach it, since section 5.4.2 gives the `state` enum its own dedicated row rather than relying on that one. Tracked as a specification gap.

CDX-E-MANIFEST-PRESENTATION-TYPE-UNKNOWN.summary: A `manifest.presentation[]` entry declares a type outside the enum.
CDX-E-MANIFEST-PRESENTATION-TYPE-UNKNOWN.dispositionClause: State Machine section 5.4.2 assigns NO row to this defect class. The projection fails closed on it (the projected bytes would otherwise be ambiguous), but the specification does not state a load-time disposition and none is inferred here — notably the generic "missing or mistyping a required field" row does not reach it, since section 5.4.2 gives the `state` enum its own dedicated row rather than relying on that one. Tracked as a specification gap.

CDX-E-MANIFEST-REFERENCE-HASH-CONFLICT.summary: The same declared path appears twice with conflicting hashes.
CDX-E-MANIFEST-REFERENCE-HASH-CONFLICT.dispositionClause: State Machine section 5.4.2 assigns NO row to this defect class. The projection fails closed on it (the projected bytes would otherwise be ambiguous), but the specification does not state a load-time disposition and none is inferred here — notably the generic "missing or mistyping a required field" row does not reach it, since section 5.4.2 gives the `state` enum its own dedicated row rather than relying on that one. Tracked as a specification gap.
CDX-E-MANIFEST-REFERENCE-HASH-CONFLICT.note: Emitted for the config-file references and the asset-index references.

CDX-E-MANIFEST-REQUIRED-SIGNER-SET-INVAL.summary: `signaturePolicy.requiredSigners` is absent, is not an array, or is empty.
CDX-E-MANIFEST-REQUIRED-SIGNER-SET-INVAL.dispositionClause: State Machine section 5.4.2 assigns NO row to this defect class. The projection fails closed on it (the projected bytes would otherwise be ambiguous), but the specification does not state a load-time disposition and none is inferred here — notably the generic "missing or mistyping a required field" row does not reach it, since section 5.4.2 gives the `state` enum its own dedicated row rather than relying on that one. Tracked as a specification gap.
CDX-E-MANIFEST-REQUIRED-SIGNER-SET-INVAL.note: An empty set is forbidden so an absent policy and an empty policy are not both expressible.

CDX-E-MANIFEST-REQUIRED-SIGNER-DUPLICATE.summary: Two `requiredSigners` entries declare the same identity.
CDX-E-MANIFEST-REQUIRED-SIGNER-DUPLICATE.dispositionClause: State Machine section 5.4.2 assigns NO row to this defect class. The projection fails closed on it (the projected bytes would otherwise be ambiguous), but the specification does not state a load-time disposition and none is inferred here — notably the generic "missing or mistyping a required field" row does not reach it, since section 5.4.2 gives the `state` enum its own dedicated row rather than relying on that one. Tracked as a specification gap.

CDX-E-MANIFEST-REQUIRED-SIGNER-KIND-AMBI.summary: A `requiredSigners` entry carries zero, or more than one, identity kind.
CDX-E-MANIFEST-REQUIRED-SIGNER-KIND-AMBI.dispositionClause: State Machine section 5.4.2 assigns NO row to this defect class. The projection fails closed on it (the projected bytes would otherwise be ambiguous), but the specification does not state a load-time disposition and none is inferred here — notably the generic "missing or mistyping a required field" row does not reach it, since section 5.4.2 gives the `state` enum its own dedicated row rather than relying on that one. Tracked as a specification gap.
CDX-E-MANIFEST-REQUIRED-SIGNER-KIND-AMBI.note: Spans both the zero-kind and multiple-kind cases: section 3.12 requires exactly one identity kind, and this implementation fails closed on either violation at a single guard.

CDX-E-MANIFEST-REQUIRED-SIGNER-ID-MALFOR.summary: A `requiredSigners` identity value is malformed for its declared kind.
CDX-E-MANIFEST-REQUIRED-SIGNER-ID-MALFOR.dispositionClause: State Machine section 5.4.2 assigns NO row to this defect class. The projection fails closed on it (the projected bytes would otherwise be ambiguous), but the specification does not state a load-time disposition and none is inferred here — notably the generic "missing or mistyping a required field" row does not reach it, since section 5.4.2 gives the `state` enum its own dedicated row rather than relying on that one. Tracked as a specification gap.

CDX-E-LINEAGE-CYCLE.summary: The lineage walk revisited a document already on the current path.
CDX-E-LINEAGE-CYCLE.dispositionClause: State Machine section 5.4.2 (REJECTED lineage — WARNING in every state)
CDX-E-LINEAGE-CYCLE.note: The lineage MUST be treated as forged — a verifier MUST NOT present any claimed ancestry as authenticated, and MUST surface the proven inconsistency as distinct from a merely unverifiable chain. The document's content identity is unaffected, so it is NOT blocked on lineage grounds: the outcome REJECTED is not the disposition REJECT.

CDX-E-LINEAGE-ANCESTORS-CONTRADICT.summary: A declared `ancestors` entry contradicts the resolved parent chain (forged tail).
CDX-E-LINEAGE-ANCESTORS-CONTRADICT.dispositionClause: State Machine section 5.4.2 (REJECTED lineage — WARNING in every state)
CDX-E-LINEAGE-ANCESTORS-CONTRADICT.note: Forged lineage: claimed ancestry MUST NOT be presented as authenticated, but the document is not blocked on lineage grounds. The outcome REJECTED is not the disposition REJECT.

CDX-E-LINEAGE-ROOT-WITH-ANCESTORS.summary: A document declaring no parent also declares a non-empty ancestors chain.
CDX-E-LINEAGE-ROOT-WITH-ANCESTORS.dispositionClause: State Machine section 5.4.2 (REJECTED lineage — WARNING in every state)
CDX-E-LINEAGE-ROOT-WITH-ANCESTORS.note: Forged lineage: claimed ancestry MUST NOT be presented as authenticated, but the document is not blocked on lineage grounds. The outcome REJECTED is not the disposition REJECT.

CDX-E-LINEAGE-UNRESOLVABLE.summary: A link in the chain could not be resolved, so the chain cannot be fully walked.
CDX-E-LINEAGE-UNRESOLVABLE.dispositionClause: State Machine section 5.4.2 (INCOMPLETE lineage — WARNING in every state)
CDX-E-LINEAGE-UNRESOLVABLE.note: Unverified, never endorsed: no ancestor beyond the break is presented as verified. An unverifiable proof never rejects the document (section 5.4.3).

CDX-E-LINEAGE-BOUND-REACHED.summary: The traversal depth or breadth bound was reached before the walk completed.
CDX-E-LINEAGE-BOUND-REACHED.dispositionClause: State Machine section 5.4.2 (INCOMPLETE lineage — WARNING in every state)
CDX-E-LINEAGE-BOUND-REACHED.note: A legitimately deep or wide history is not an inconsistency.

CDX-E-ARCHIVE-UNREADABLE.summary: The archive cannot be parsed as a ZIP: no End Of Central Directory record, or a truncated or malformed central directory.
CDX-E-ARCHIVE-UNREADABLE.dispositionClause: State Machine section 5.4.2 (Archive unreadable ... REJECT in every state)

CDX-E-ARCHIVE-DUPLICATE-ENTRY.summary: Two central-directory entries resolve to the same path after the section 3.4 normalization.
CDX-E-ARCHIVE-DUPLICATE-ENTRY.dispositionClause: State Machine section 5.4.3 (Duplicate archive entries always REJECT, in every state)
CDX-E-ARCHIVE-DUPLICATE-ENTRY.note: Closes a split-view substitution: a signer's tooling hashes one entry into the document id while a victim's reader materializes a different entry of the same name under one still-valid signature.

CDX-E-ARCHIVE-CASE-COLLISION.summary: Two central-directory entries differ only in case, colliding when extracted onto a case-insensitive filesystem.
CDX-E-ARCHIVE-CASE-COLLISION.dispositionClause: State Machine section 5.4.3 (Duplicate archive entries, including a case-only collision, always REJECT, in every state)

CDX-E-ARCHIVE-LFH-CD-DISAGREEMENT.summary: The local-file-header view and the central-directory view disagree on the entry set: a central entry with no matching local header, or a local name that differs from its central name.
CDX-E-ARCHIVE-LFH-CD-DISAGREEMENT.dispositionClause: State Machine section 5.4.3 (local-file-header/central-directory entry-set disagreement always REJECTs, in every state)

CDX-E-ARCHIVE-DATA-OUTSIDE-CD.summary: A local file header exists that the central directory does not enumerate (file data outside the authoritative index).
CDX-E-ARCHIVE-DATA-OUTSIDE-CD.dispositionClause: State Machine section 5.4.3 (local-file-header/central-directory entry-set disagreement always REJECTs, in every state)
CDX-E-ARCHIVE-DATA-OUTSIDE-CD.note: The central directory is the authoritative index; a stray local entry is a split-view substitution vector.

CDX-E-ARCHIVE-UNSAFE-NAME.summary: An archive entry name fails the section 9.1 safety checks: a `..` segment, an absolute or drive/UNC/colon-bearing name, a backslash, a reserved Windows device name, a trailing dot/space segment, or an overlong or ill-formed UTF-8 encoding.
CDX-E-ARCHIVE-UNSAFE-NAME.dispositionClause: State Machine section 5.4.3 (an unsafe archive path or symlink always REJECTs, in every state)

CDX-E-ARCHIVE-SYMLINK-ENTRY.summary: An archive entry encodes a symbolic link (a Unix mode with the symlink bit set).
CDX-E-ARCHIVE-SYMLINK-ENTRY.dispositionClause: State Machine section 5.4.3 (an unsafe archive path or symlink always REJECTs, in every state)
CDX-E-ARCHIVE-SYMLINK-ENTRY.note: CDX defines no legitimate use for a symlink entry; it is a second path-traversal vector.

CDX-E-ARCHIVE-DECOMPRESSION-BOMB.summary: A declared entry size, decompression ratio, or archive total exceeds the reader's finite bound.
CDX-E-ARCHIVE-DECOMPRESSION-BOMB.dispositionClause: Container Format sections 5.3 and 9.2 (a conforming reader MUST enforce a finite bound on every attacker-controlled dimension and MUST fail closed -- reject the archive -- when one is exceeded). Section 5.4.2 does not itself enumerate this class; the disposition is stated in the mechanism sections.
CDX-E-ARCHIVE-DECOMPRESSION-BOMB.note: The specific thresholds are implementation-defined (section 5.1 gives reasonable defaults); the existence and enforcement of a bound is the requirement. A fixture bomb must exceed any plausible conformant bound, not one specific threshold.

CDX-E-ARCHIVE-CRC-MISMATCH.summary: A stored ZIP CRC-32 does not match the CRC recomputed over the entry's actual bytes (a corrupt entry).
CDX-E-ARCHIVE-CRC-MISMATCH.dispositionClause: State Machine section 5.4.2 (ZIP CRC-32 mismatch for an archive entry). The stored bytes cannot be trusted to decompress to the content they claim, independently of the semantic document hash (section 6.2).

CDX-E-ARCHIVE-FIRST-FILE-NOT-MANIFEST.summary: The first archive entry is not `manifest.json`.
CDX-E-ARCHIVE-FIRST-FILE-NOT-MANIFEST.dispositionClause: State Machine section 5.4.2 (the first archive entry is not manifest.json). The archive is fully readable and the manifest resolvable by name, so only the streaming/quick-validation optimization is lost.

CDX-E-ARCHIVE-ENCRYPTION-USED.summary: An entry uses ZIP-level encryption (general-purpose bit 0 set); CDX uses the security extension for encryption, never the container.
CDX-E-ARCHIVE-ENCRYPTION-USED.dispositionClause: State Machine section 5.4.2 (ZIP-level encryption present, or a multi-volume archive, both forbidden by Container Format section 3.1 and neither coherently loadable)

CDX-E-ARCHIVE-MULTI-VOLUME.summary: The archive is a multi-volume (split) ZIP: an End Of Central Directory disk field is non-zero.
CDX-E-ARCHIVE-MULTI-VOLUME.dispositionClause: State Machine section 5.4.2 (ZIP-level encryption present, or a multi-volume archive, both forbidden by Container Format section 3.1 and neither coherently loadable)
